inputs.label: Fuel Type
inputs.businessContext: Fuel type determines heating value and combustion characteristics. Based on ASME PTC 4 and ISO 50001.

inputs.label: Fuel Consumption
inputs.businessContext: Mass or volumetric flow rate of fuel input. Essential for energy balance.

inputs.label: Fuel Heating Value (HHV)
inputs.businessContext: Higher Heating Value of fuel. Standard test methods ASTM D240 for liquids, ASTM D5865 for solids.

inputs.label: Steam Flow Rate
inputs.businessContext: Mass flow rate of steam produced. Key output parameter.

inputs.label: Steam Enthalpy
inputs.businessContext: Enthalpy of steam at operating pressure and temperature. From steam tables per ASME.

inputs.label: Feedwater Enthalpy
inputs.businessContext: Enthalpy of feedwater entering boiler. Typically at deaerator outlet temperature.

inputs.label: Excess Air Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Excess air used for combustion. Affects flue gas losses. Typical range 10-50%.

inputs.label: Flue Gas Exit Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Temperature of flue gases leaving boiler. Higher temperature increases losses.

inputs.label: Ambient Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Reference ambient temperature for heat loss calculations.

inputs.label: Radiation and Convection Loss
inputs.businessContext: Heat loss from boiler surface. Typically 1-3% of input energy.

inputs.label: Blowdown Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of boiler water discharged to control solids. Affects efficiency.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: User's confidence in input data accuracy. Adjusts output reliability.

thresholds.excessAir: excessAir > 50 -> 'Warning: Excess air too high, increase heat loss'
thresholds.flueGasTemperature: flueGasTemperature > 300 -> 'Warning: High flue gas temperature, consider economizer'

suggestedActions: Reduce excess air to optimal level (10-20%)
Install economizer to recover flue gas heat
Optimize blowdown rate based on water quality
Insulate boiler surface to reduce radiation loss
